Part B.key_evidence: Difference image shows a clear dipole/yin-yang pattern (adjacent black/white residuals) at the candidate location. The science and reference both show two bright sources, and the residual indicates a positional or PSF mismatch rather than a clean point-source brightening. ndethist=1 with ncovhist=765 means this position has been visited many times but only detected once. Nearest PS1 source is bright (r=16.6) at 2.88 arcsec with sgscore1=0.55 (ambiguous).
Part B.leading_interpretation_and_support: The dipole residual in the difference image is the hallmark of a subtraction artifact (astrometric/PSF mismatch on a bright nearby star). With 765 prior coverages and only this single detection, plus a poor subtraction morphology, the alert is most consistent with a bogus/artifact detection.
Part B.alternative_analysis: An alternative is a genuine transient (e.g., supernova) near the bright PS1 source, since distpsnr1~2.9 arcsec could place it near a host. However, the difference morphology is not a clean positive PSF residual but a clear dipole, disfavoring a real astrophysical brightening.
